questions.q: Analysis of over 400,000 Reddit posts on GLP-1 medications revealed potential side effects not captured in clinical trials. Which of the following was NOT among the reported side effects?
questions.correctText: Gallbladder issues were not explicitly mentioned as a side effect in the analysis of Reddit posts, though gastrointestinal issues were common.

questions.q: According to a recent study, summers in the midlatitudes, including much of North America and Europe, now last approximately how much longer than they did in the 1960s?
questions.correctText: Summers in the midlatitudes now last roughly 30 days longer than they did in the 1960s.

questions.q: New research suggests that regulatory T cells (Tregs), which act as immune system 'brakes,' are often altered in children with what condition?
questions.correctText: Regulatory T cells are altered in children with autism, potentially playing a role in neuroinflammation and behavior.

questions.q: A study analyzing the global wildlife trade found that traded mammals are how much more likely to share pathogens with humans than non-traded mammals?
questions.correctText: Traded mammals are 1.5 times more likely to share pathogens with humans than non-traded species.

questions.q: Research using flight path data and Bayesian dynamical systems learning has helped predict how mosquitoes target humans. What two main stimuli were identified as crucial for mosquitoes approaching their targets?
questions.correctText: Visual cues and carbon dioxide are the two main stimuli identified as crucial for mosquitoes targeting humans.
